channel.title: AI English Daily
channel.description: Daily AI news delivered as business English presentations. Curated for data scientists and engineering managers. Practice shadowing, expand vocabulary, master presentation frameworks.
channel.itunes:author: Kenichiro Nishioka
channel.itunes:summary: Daily AI news delivered as business English presentations.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/21
item.description:
Today's framework: What → So What → Now What — State the facts, explain why they matter, then say what to do next.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic (Claude Blog): Claude Managed Agents — self-hosted sandboxes and MCP tunnels keep tool execution and internal systems inside the customer's security perimeter. • VentureBeat: Gemini 3.5 Flash GA — a frontier-level model at $1.50/$9 per 1M tokens with a 1M context window and ~4x speed changes the cost math for agents. • Help Net Security: AI agents in production — a confused-deputy problem and a propose-commit split for safe write access to infrastructure.
✨ Featured expressions: decoupling the agent's brain from its hands so to speak, fundamentally changes the cost math, now is the time to re-evaluate (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: orchestration //ˌɔːrkɪˈstreɪʃən//, telemetry //təˈlemətri//, security perimeter //sɪˈkjʊərəti pəˈrɪmɪtər//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/20
item.description:
Today's framework: Before → After → Bridge — Contrast the old state, the new state, and the transition that connects them.
📰 Stories covered: • Google Blog: I/O 2026 agentic Gemini era — Google reported massive Gemini usage and positioned agents as a product-platform shift. • Google Blog: I/O announcements — Gemini Omni, Gemini 3.5 Flash, and Antigravity push AI toward multimodal action and agent-first development. • AWS ML Blog: Frontier agents — AWS made Security Agent and DevOps Agent generally available for security testing and operations. • LangChain Blog: LangSmith Engine — LangChain explained an agent that turns traces and failures into issues, evaluators, and fixes. • OpenAI: Content provenance — OpenAI added C2PA conformance, SynthID watermarking, and a public image verification preview.
✨ Featured expressions: turn impressive AI features into dependable business infrastructure, take responsibility for a workflow, the operating load of an AI platform (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: provenance //ˈprɒvənəns//, agentic //eɪˈdʒentɪk//, penetration testing //ˌpenəˈtreɪʃən ˈtestɪŋ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/19
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem → Solution → Impact — Define the core business problem, explain the practical solution, then show the operational impact.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: OpenAI and Dell Technologies partner to bring Codex to hybrid and... — OpenAI and Dell Technologies announced a collaboration to help enterprises deploy Codex... • Anthropic: Anthropic acquires Stainless — Anthropic announced it is acquiring Stainless, whose tooling generates SDKs, CLIs, and MCP... • GitHub Blog: Take your local GitHub sessions anywhere — GitHub made remote control for Copilot CLI sessions generally available on github.com and... • Hugging Face / IBM Research: The Open Agent Leaderboard — Hugging Face and IBM Research launched the Open Agent Leaderboard and Exgentic framework to... • Google Gemini Help / PCWorld: Google shifts Gemini usage limits toward compute-based quotas — Google updated Gemini usage guidance around compute-based limits, with usage affected by...
✨ Featured expressions: the operating model around them is still catching up, the quality of the bridges around it, measurement discipline (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: hybrid deployment /ˈhaɪbrɪd dɪˈplɔɪmənt/, data governance /ˈdeɪtə ˈɡʌvərnəns/, connector layer /kəˈnɛktər ˈleɪər/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/18
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point → Reason → Example → Point: lead with the takeaway, justify it, support it with examples, then restate the takeaway.
📰 Stories covered: • Meta Newsroom: Introducing a Completely Private Way to Chat With AI — Meta launched Incognito Chat with Meta AI on WhatsApp and the Meta AI app, built on WhatsApp Private Processing. • Notion Blog: Introducing Notion’s Developer Platform — Notion introduced a Developer Platform with Workers for hosted custom code, External Agents that appear as native workspace participants, an External Agent API for bringing internal agents into Notion, and the ntn CLI for terminal and IDE workflows. • OpenAI Codex Changelog: Work with Codex from anywhere — OpenAI added the ability to use Codex from the ChatGPT mobile app by connecting to a Mac running the Codex app.
✨ Featured expressions: marked by a strong emphasis on, coupled with an increasing focus on, The practical takeaway is that (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: Incognito Chat /ɪnˈkɒɡnɪtoʊ tʃæt/, Private Processing /ˈpraɪvət ˈprɑːsesɪŋ/, External Agents /ɪkˈstɜːrnəl ˈeɪdʒənts/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/17
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Frame the current state, surface the tension, then close with the practical resolution.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: Databricks brings GPT-5.5 to enterprise agent workflows — Databricks is making GPT-5. • CIO Dive: xAI joins crowded coding agent race with Grok Build — xAI unveiled Grok Build, an early-beta agentic command-line interface for software engineering and complex coding, available to SuperGrok Heavy subscribers. • Cursor Changelog: Development environments for cloud agents — Cursor introduced configurable development environments for cloud agents, including multi-repo environments, Dockerfile-based configuration, build secrets, faster layer caching, version history, audit logs, and scoped egress and secrets. • LangChain Blog: New in Deep Agents v0.6 — LangChain’s Deep Agents v0. • Sebastian Raschka: Recent Developments in LLM Architectures: KV Sharing, mHC, and Compressed Attention — Sebastian Raschka reviewed recent open-weight LLM architecture changes such as KV sharing in Gemma 4, compressed convolutional attention in ZAYA1, attention budgeting in Laguna XS.
✨ Featured expressions: moving beyond mere conversational interfaces, raw conversational prowess, a strategic battleground (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: orchestration /ˌɔːrkəˈstreɪʃən/, checkpoint storage /ˈtʃekpɔɪnt ˈstɔːrɪdʒ/, command-line interface /kəˈmænd laɪn ˈɪntərfeɪs/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/16
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Set the business context, explain the risk or tension, then close with practical next steps.
📰 Stories covered: • AWS Machine Learning Blog: AWS launches frontier agents for security testing and cloud operations — AWS announced general availability of AWS Security Agent for on-demand penetration testing and AWS DevOps Agent for autonomous operational workflows across AWS, multicloud, and on-premises environments. • Visual Studio Code Blog: The Coding Harness Behind GitHub Copilot in VS Code — The VS Code team published a deep dive on the coding harness behind GitHub Copilot, describing how it assembles context, exposes tools, validates tool calls, runs the agent loop, and feeds observations back to the model. • xAI Docs: Grok Model Retirement on May 15, 2026 — xAI retired several earlier Grok API model slugs effective May 15, automatically redirecting requests to Grok 4.3 or a newer image model and warning developers about pricing differences.
✨ Featured expressions: high-stakes workflows, the visible API call looks stable, remove bottlenecks without removing accountability (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: penetration testing /ˌpenɪˈtreɪʃən ˈtestɪŋ/, incident resolution /ˈɪnsɪdənt ˌrezəˈluːʃən/, model slug /ˈmɑːdəl slʌɡ/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/15
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Frame the current context, explain the tension, then close with practical actions.
📰 Stories covered: • xAI Docs: Grok Model Retirement on May 15, 2026 — xAI is retiring several older Grok API model slugs on May 15 and automatically redirecting them to Grok 4. • GitHub Blog: Changes to GitHub Copilot Individual plans — GitHub updated its Copilot Individual plan changes: new Pro, Pro+, and Student sign-ups are paused, usage limits are tightened, model availability changes, and VS Code plus Copilot CLI now show approaching usage limits. • LangChain Blog: New in Deep Agents v0.6 — LangChain released Deep Agents v0. • Cursor Changelog: Development environments for cloud agents — Cursor introduced configurable development environments for cloud agents, including multi-repo environments, Dockerfile-based configuration, build secrets, faster layer caching, validation, version history, audit logs, and environment-level secret scoping.
✨ Featured expressions: the center of gravity is moving, production hygiene, financially legible (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: model slug /ˈmɑːdəl slʌɡ/, reasoning effort /ˈriːzənɪŋ ˈefərt/, agentic workflow /eɪˈdʒentɪk ˈwɜːrkfloʊ/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/14
item.description:
Today's framework: What→So What→Now What — Separate the facts, the business implications, and the next actions.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic News: Introducing Claude for Small Business — Anthropic packaged Claude into small-business workflows across everyday tools. • Cursor Changelog: Development environments for cloud agents — Cursor added controlled cloud-agent environments with multi-repo setup, secrets, caching, audit logs, and scoped access. • LangChain Blog: Introducing LangSmith Engine — LangChain launched a beta system that turns production traces into clustered issues, fixes, and eval coverage. • LangChain Blog: LangSmith Sandboxes are Generally Available — LangChain made isolated microVM sandboxes generally available for model-generated code execution. • LangChain Blog: Introducing Managed Deep Agents — LangChain introduced a hosted runtime for durable, long-running agents with context, tools, tracing, and approval.
✨ Featured expressions: the operating layer, remove operational friction, the control surface is mature enough (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: cloud agents /klaʊd ˈeɪdʒənts/, production traces /prəˈdʌkʃən treɪsɪz/, evaluator /ɪˈvæljueɪtər/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/13
item.description:
Today's framework: Before → After → Bridge — Contrast the old state with the new state, then connect both to the practical transition plan.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: OpenAI launches the OpenAI Deployment Company to help businesses build around intelligence — OpenAI launched the OpenAI Deployment Company, agreed to acquire Tomoro, and said the new unit will embed Forward Deployed Engineers into organizations to build production AI systems around critical workflows. • Google Blog: Gemini Intelligence brings proactive AI to Android — At The Android Show 2026, Google introduced Gemini Intelligence, a set of proactive Android AI features intended to automate tasks across apps, generate custom widgets, refine spoken messages, and expand to phones, wearables, cars, glasses, and laptops. • The Hacker News: OpenAI Launches Daybreak for AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection and Patch Validation — The Hacker News reported that OpenAI launched Daybreak, a cybersecurity initiative for secure code review, threat modeling, vulnerability testing, patch validation, dependency risk analysis, and remediation guidance. • The Hacker News: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages — A Mini Shai-Hulud campaign compromised npm and PyPI packages tied to TanStack, UiPath, Mistral AI, OpenSearch, and Guardrails AI, adding credential-stealing malware and IDE persistence hooks.
✨ Featured expressions: hands-on operating-model change, pull-based, cross-application delegation (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agentic //eɪˈdʒen.tɪk//, deployment //dɪˈplɔɪ.mənt//, threat modeling //θret ˈmɑː.dəl.ɪŋ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/12
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem → Solution → Impact — Frame the business bottleneck, show the operating solution, then clarify the practical impact.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI Blog: OpenAI launches the OpenAI Deployment Company to help businesses build around intelligence — OpenAI launched a majority-controlled Deployment Company with more than $4 billion of initial investment. • Anthropic News: Agents for financial services — Anthropic released ten ready-to-run financial-services agent templates. • AWS News Blog: AWS Weekly Roundup: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments, Agent Toolkit for AWS, and more — AWS highlighted Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments in preview. • Anthropic News: Higher usage limits for Claude and a compute deal with SpaceX — Anthropic announced a SpaceX partnership for access to more than 300 megawatts of capacity.
✨ Featured expressions: deployment bottleneck, necessary, but not sufficient, inside policy boundaries (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: deployment //dɪˈplɔɪmənt//, agent template //ˈeɪdʒənt ˈtɛmplət//, audit trail //ˈɔːdɪt treɪl//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/11
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point → Reason → Example → Point: state your claim, justify it, illustrate it, then drive it home.
📰 Stories covered: • LangChain Blog: Building a company due diligence agent — Five parallel subagents cover corporate profile, financials, litigation, reputation, and competitors, then fan out per-competitor analysis with isolated contexts and per-field citations. • OpenAI Engineering: Advancing voice intelligence — GPT-Realtime-2 (GPT-5-class reasoning), GPT-Realtime-Translate (70+ input languages), and GPT-Realtime-Whisper (streaming transcription) shift voice from UI layer to app runtime. • OpenAI: Testing ads in ChatGPT — Expanded to UK, Japan, South Korea; Anthropic stays ad-free. The business model split is now concrete.
✨ Featured expressions: strategic fork in the road, compelling differentiator, fan-out orchestration (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: due diligence /djuː ˈdɪlɪdʒəns/, orchestrator /ˈɔːkɪstreɪtər/, calibrated confidence score /ˈkælɪbreɪtɪd/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/10
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Frame any challenge as current state, the tension it creates, and how it gets resolved.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: Testing ads in ChatGPT — expanded to UK, Japan, South Korea and more — OpenAI announced it will expand its ChatGPT advertising pilot to the UK, Mexico, Brazil, Japan, and South Korea in the coming weeks. • LangChain Blog: The Agent Development Lifecycle — LangChain CEO Harrison Chase published a framework for production agent development organized around four phases: Build → Test → Deploy → Monitor. • OpenAI: Introducing Trusted Contact in ChatGPT — OpenAI launched 'Trusted Contact', an optional feature in ChatGPT that allows adults to nominate a friend, family member, or caregiver who may be notified if automated systems and human reviewers detect the enrolled person may be in acute distress.
✨ Featured expressions: dive into, in practice, think through (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: metrics //ˈmetrɪks//, taxonomy //tækˈsɒnəmi//, governance //ˈɡʌvərnəns//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/09
item.description:
Today's framework: Before → After → Bridge — Set the scene before the change, describe what's different now, then connect it to what you should do next.
📰 Stories covered: • Cursor Changelog: PR Review, Build Plan in Parallel, and Split PRs — Cursor ships native in-IDE PR review, parallel async subagent plan execution, and auto-split-to-PRs, covering the full loop from writing code to shipping it. • OpenAI: Scaling Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber — GPT-5.5-Cyber launches in limited preview for critical infrastructure defenders with identity-verified access and lower refusal rates for defensive security workflows. • OpenAI Security: Running Codex safely at OpenAI — OpenAI publishes their internal Codex deployment blueprint: sandboxed execution, managed network policies, auto-approval for low-risk actions, and OpenTelemetry agent-intent logs. • Hugging Face Blog (AllenAI): EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity — AllenAI's new MoE pretraining method lets experts specialize organically, reducing the manual routing-tuning burden that has slowed frontier model development.
✨ Featured expressions: serial task queuing, the upshot is, said another way (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: async subagent /ˈeɪsɪŋk ˈsʌbeɪdʒənt/, identity-gated /aɪˈdentɪti ˈɡeɪtɪd/, sandboxed execution /ˈsændbɒkst ɪkˈsekjuːʃən/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/08
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation-Complication-Resolution — Set the scene, introduce the tension, then resolve with insight or action.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI Engineering: Advancing voice intelligence with new models in the API — OpenAI launched three new realtime voice models via API: GPT-Realtime-2 (GPT-5-class reasoning in live voice, 128K context, parallel tool calls), GPT-Realtime-Translate (live speech translation across 70+ input languages into 13 output languages), and GPT-Realtime-Whisper (streaming speech-to-text transcription as the speaker talks). • Mozilla Hacks: Behind the Scenes Hardening Firefox with Claude Mythos Preview — Mozilla published a detailed technical post on how they used Claude Mythos Preview (plus other AI models) to find and fix an unprecedented number of latent security vulnerabilities in Firefox. • Simon Willison: Notes on the xAI/Anthropic data center deal — Anthropic announced at the Code w/ Claude event that they are leasing all capacity of Colossus 1, xAI/SpaceX's Memphis data center, to address compute constraints.
✨ Featured expressions: Let's dive into, paving the way for, To put that in context (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: context window //ˈkɒntekst ˈwɪndoʊ//, latent security vulnerabilities //ˈleɪtənt sɪˈkjʊərɪtiː ˌvʌlnərəˈbɪlɪtiz//, compute constraints //kəmˈpjuːt kənˈstreɪnts//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/07
item.description:
Today's framework: What → So What → Now What — State the facts, draw the implications, then give actionable next steps.
📰 Stories covered: • Simon Willison: Code w/ Claude 2026: Anthropic launches multi-agent orchestration, Dreaming, and SpaceX Colossus partnership — At Anthropic's Code w/ Claude developer event, the company announced three new features for Claude Managed Agents: multi-agent orchestration to deploy fleets of specialized agents on complex tasks; Outcomes, which lets you define what success looks like so Claude iterates until it gets there; and Dreaming, an overnight mode where Claude replays previous sessions to synthesize new memories and self-generated playbooks. • OpenAI: How frontier enterprises are building an AI advantage — introducing B2B Signals — OpenAI published the inaugural B2B Signals report, a recurring study of enterprise AI adoption using aggregated, de-identified usage data. • OpenAI Engineering: Supercomputer networking to accelerate large-scale AI training — OpenAI open-sources MRC — OpenAI released MRC (Multipath Reliable Connection), a novel GPU networking protocol co-developed with AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft, and NVIDIA, through the Open Compute Project. • Simon Willison: Vibe coding and agentic engineering are getting closer than I'd like — Simon Willison reflects on a realization from a recent podcast interview: the boundary between vibe coding (generating code without reviewing it) and agentic engineering (using AI tools with full professional rigor) is blurring in his own work.
✨ Featured expressions: To put that in context, The short version is that, What this really means is that (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: Multi-agent orchestration //ˌmʌlti ˈeɪdʒənt ˌɔːrkeˈstreɪʃən//, Agentic engineering //eɪˈdʒentɪk ˌendʒɪˈnɪərɪŋ//, Frontier firms //frʌnˈtɪər fɜːrmz//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/06
item.description:
Today's framework: Before→After→Bridge — Set the scene, show what changed, then connect it to your work
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: GPT-5.5 Instant: smarter, clearer, and more personalized — OpenAI replaced GPT-5.3 Instant with GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's default model for all users. The update delivers 52.5%. • LangChain Blog: Agent observability needs feedback to power learning — LangChain founder Harrison Chase published an essay arguing that agent observability must go beyond trace debugging to p. • Cursor Changelog: Model controls, spend management, and usage analytics — Cursor released a set of Enterprise admin features: granular model access controls letting admins allowlist or blocklist.
✨ Featured expressions: undercut the efficiency gains, a recurring obstacle to [X], immediately actionable, lower the barrier to entry (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: hallucination //ˌhæl.juːˌsɪn.ˈeɪ.ʃən//, observability //ˌɒb.zɜː.vəˈbɪl.ɪ.ti//, harness //ˈhɑː.nɪs//, governance //ˈɡʌv.ən.əns//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/05
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem → Solution → Impact — Frame each story as a challenge, the industry response, and the real-world consequences
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI Engineering: How OpenAI delivers low-latency voice AI at scale — Rebuilt WebRTC stack with split relay + transceiver architecture to serve 900M+ users with crisp, low-latency voice AI. • Vercel Engineering: Introducing deepsec — Open-source AI security harness using Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 that runs on your own infra, scales to 1,000+ sandboxes, 10–20% false-positive rate. • AWS ML Blog: AgentCore Optimization now in preview — Closed-loop agent improvement: production traces → recommendations → batch eval / A/B testing → ship with statistical confidence.
✨ Featured expressions: silent quality drift, closed-loop system, experiment-driven engineering (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: WebRTC /ˌwɛb ˌɑːr tiː ˈsiː/, transceiver architecture /trænsˈsiːvər ˈɑːrkɪtektʃər/, production traces /prəˈdʌkʃən ˈtreɪsɪz/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/04
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point → Reason → Example → Point: state the takeaway, justify it, ground it with evidence, then reinforce.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic Research: How people ask Claude for personal guidance — Anthropic analyzed 1 million Claude conversations using a privacy-preserving tool, finding that roug. • AWS ML Blog: Configuring Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway for secure access to private resources — AWS announced and documented VPC egress support for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway — enabling AI a.
✨ Featured expressions: What this means in practice is, To put that in context, In essence (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: sycophancy /ˈsɪk.ə.fən.si/, empirical /ɪmˈpɪr.ɪ.kəl/, VPC egress /viː piː siː ˈiː.ɡres/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/03
item.description:
Today's framework: What → So What → Now What — Describe what happened, explain why it matters, then define what to do next.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI News: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS — OpenAI and AWS expanded their partnership, putting GPT-5.5 and Codex on Amazon Bedrock so enterprise teams can build on OpenAI inside their existing AWS infrastructure. • OpenAI Company: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership — Microsoft's license shifts from exclusive to non-exclusive, and the same-week AWS deal reveals a deliberate multi-cloud distribution strategy. • Cursor: Build programmatic agents with the Cursor SDK — @cursor/sdk lets developers build coding agents programmatically using the same runtime that powers Cursor, turning IDE intelligence into a composable API.
✨ Featured expressions: lower the barrier to entry, reducing vendor lock-in risks, first-ship rights (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: non-exclusive license /ˌnɒn ɪkˈskluːsɪv ˈlaɪsəns/, composable API /kəmˈpoʊzəbl ˌeɪ piː ˈaɪ/, multi-cloud strategy /ˈmʌlti klaʊd ˈstrætədʒi/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/02
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point — Reason — Evidence — Point (Recap): state the claim, justify it, back it up, and land it.
📰 Stories covered: • Cursor Changelog: Cursor Security Review in Beta: Always-On AI Security Agents for Teams — Cursor launched Security Review in beta for Teams and Enterprise plans. It ships two always-on agents: Security Reviewer... • OpenAI Codex CLI (via Simon Willison): Codex CLI 0.128.0 Adds /goal: Agent Loops Until the Job Is Done — OpenAI shipped Codex CLI version 0.128.0 with a /goal command. Users set a high-level goal (e.g., 'add full test coverag... • Cursor Changelog: Cursor Team Marketplace: Centralized MCP Plugin Distribution for Engineering Teams — Cursor now allows admins to create a team plugin marketplace without first connecting a repository. Plugins bundle capab... • UK AI Security Institute: UK AISI Evaluates GPT-5.5: Comparable to Claude Mythos in Cyber Capabilities—and Publicly Available — The UK AI Security Institute evaluated GPT-5.5 for offensive cybersecurity capabilities—specifically its ability to find...
✨ Featured expressions: In other words, To put that in context, What this means in practice (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: pull request /pʊl rɪˈkwest/, prompt injection /prɒmpt ɪnˈdʒekʃən/, Model Context Protocol /ˈmɒdəl ˈkɒntekst ˈprəʊtəkɒl/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/01
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation→Complication→Resolution — Identify the situation, expose the complication, then deliver the resolution.
📰 Stories covered: • Google DeepMind Blog: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician — Google DeepMind announced its AI co-clinician research initiative, designed to act as a collaborative member of a clinical care team under physician supervision. • OpenAI News: Introducing Advanced Account Security — OpenAI launched Advanced Account Security, an opt-in setting that requires passkeys or physical security keys (disabling passwords), disables email/SMS recovery in favor of backup passkeys and recovery keys, shortens session windows, and automatically excludes conversations from model training. • AWS ML Blog: Run custom MCP proxies serverless on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime — AWS published a guide and open-source implementation for deploying a serverless MCP proxy on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime.
✨ Featured expressions: a testament to its accuracy and reliability, the margin for error is virtually nonexistent, commensurate with the high stakes involved (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: triadic care model //traɪˈædɪk kɛr ˈmɒdəl//, dual-agent safety architecture //djuːəl ˈeɪdʒənt ˈseɪfti ˈɑːkɪtɛktʃər//, credential stuffing //krɪˈdɛnʃəl ˈstʌfɪŋ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/30
item.description:
Today's framework: What → So What → Now What — Present the facts, unpack the significance, then map out concrete next steps.
📰 Stories covered: • Mistral AI: Remote agents in Vibe + Medium 3.5 — Mistral launched a new mid-tier model and remote coding agents that run entirely in their cloud, no local hardware needed. • Hugging Face: AI evals are the new compute bottleneck — The HAL team spent $40K on agent rollouts; scaffold choice alone creates a 33x cost spread. • Simon Willison: LLM 0.32a0 — Major refactor aligns the popular OSS LLM library with vendor API message formats and typed outputs. • IBM Research: Granite 4.1 — Open Apache 2.0 dense LLMs (3B/8B/30B) with 512K context; the 8B model matches the previous 32B MoE model.
✨ Featured expressions: "What this signals is", "To put that in context", "The bigger picture here" (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: remote coding agents /rɪˈmoʊt ˈkoʊdɪŋ ˈeɪdʒənts/, evaluation scaffold /ɪˌvæljuˈeɪʃən ˈskæfəld/, context window /ˈkɒntekst ˈwɪndoʊ/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/29
item.description:
Today's framework: Before→After→Bridge — Map the friction of yesterday to the opportunity of today, then act.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI News: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS — OpenAI and AWS expanded their partnership to bring GPT-5.5, Codex, and Managed Agents natively to Amazon Bedrock in limited preview, letting enterprises access OpenAI's stack within their AWS security perimeter. • Cursor Changelog: Build programmatic agents with the Cursor SDK — Cursor released a TypeScript SDK in public beta giving programmatic access to its agent runtime, enabling CI/CD and automation use cases beyond the IDE. • Hugging Face Blog: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni — An open-weights omni-modal model leading benchmarks across documents, GUI, video, and audio with 9.2x throughput gains on video workloads. • Mistral AI: Workflows for work that runs the business — Mistral's Python-based AI orchestration layer enters public preview with durable execution, observability, and human-in-the-loop approvals already in production at ASML and CMA-CGM.
✨ Featured expressions: lower the barrier to entry, within the security perimeter, bridge the gap between X and Y (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: security perimeter /sɪˈkjʊərɪti pəˈrɪmɪtər/, agent runtime /ˈeɪdʒənt ˈrʌntaɪm/, human-in-the-loop /ˈhjuːmən ɪn ðə luːp/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/28
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem→Solution→Impact — Identify the core challenge, present the breakthrough, then quantify the real-world effect.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI Engineering: An open-source spec for Codex orchestration: Symphony — OpenAI released Symphony, an open-source spec and agent orchestrator that connects a project-management board (like Linear) to a fleet of continuously running Codex agents. • OpenAI Company: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership — OpenAI and Microsoft announced an amended partnership agreement. • OpenAI News: Introducing GPT-5.5 — GPT-5.
✨ Featured expressions: nothing short of transformative, human-in-the-loop model, seismic shift (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agent orchestrator //ˈeɪdʒənt ˈɔːrkɪstreɪtər//, control plane //kənˈtroʊl pleɪn//, AGI clause //ˌeɪ dʒiː ˈaɪ klɔːz//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/27
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point → Reason → Example → Point. Lead with the takeaway, support it, ground it in examples, then restate the point.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI News: Introducing GPT-5.5 — OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Codex, positioning it as a faster and more capable model for coding, computer use, research, and long-horizon work. • Hugging Face Blog: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use — Hugging Face analyzed DeepSeek-V4, highlighting 1M-token context models that cut per-token FLOPs and KV-cache requirements enough to make long agent traces more realistic. • OpenAI News: Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT — OpenAI introduced shared workspace agents for long-running organizational workflows in ChatGPT and Slack.
✨ Featured expressions: To put that in context, Put differently, What this means is (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: orchestration overhead /ˌɔːrkɪˈstreɪʃən ˈoʊvərhed/, long-horizon work /ˌlɔːŋ həˈraɪzən wɝːk/, persistent agent memory /pərˈsɪstənt ˈeɪdʒənt ˈmeməri/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/26
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Set the context, surface the tension or change, then close with the practical resolution.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic News: Anthropic and NEC collaborate to build Japan’s largest AI engineering workforce — Anthropic said NEC will deploy Claude across roughly 30,000 employees, build a Japan-based center of excellence, and jointly ship domain-specific AI products for finance, manufacturing, local government, and cybersecurity. • Windsurf Changelog: Wave 13: Merry Shipmas — Windsurf rolled out first-class support for parallel multi-agent sessions, Git worktrees, side-by-side Cascade panes, and made SWE-1.5 Free the default model for all users for the next three months. • Anthropic News: An update on our election safeguards — Anthropic detailed new evaluation methodology, political-bias measurements, public datasets, and external review partnerships meant to keep Claude balanced and accurate on election-related questions.
✨ Featured expressions: "let's dive into today's AI updates", "To put that in context, NEC is establishing a dedicated Japan-based center of excellence", "What this signifies is a concrete signal that AI coding and workflow agents are transitioning from mere pilot mode into large-scale organizational adoption" (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: center of excellence /ˌsɛntər əv ˈɛksələns/, Domain-specific AI products /ˈdoʊmeɪn spɪˈsɪfɪk eɪ aɪ ˈprɒdʌkts/, pilot mode /ˈpaɪlət moʊd/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/25
item.description: A business-style English briefing on today's AI developments, focused on model capability, agent infrastructure, and developer workflow changes. Practice presentation phrasing while following five selected AI stories.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/24
item.description: A Friday business briefing on GPT-5.5, workspace agents, privacy filtering, faster agent loops, and OpenAI’s new bio bug bounty. Practice presentation English while tracking what these releases mean for agents, privacy, and developer workflows.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/23
item.description: Today's briefing uses the What → So What → Now What framework to unpack five AI updates around workspace agents, faster agent loops, cloud agent tooling, custom TPU infrastructure, and edge multimodal assistants. The episode is designed for business-English listening practice with practical presentation phrasing and technical vocabulary in context.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/22
item.description: Today's thesis: AI is rapidly industrializing. Before/After/Bridge framework maps five enterprise pain points — change management, output quality, governance, tool-testing, and retrieval — to the week's concrete announcements from OpenAI, AWS, and Hugging Face.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/21 [Shadowing]
item.description: Shadowing practice version — same content at a measured pace (Charon voice). For pronunciation and rhythm practice.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/21
item.description: Today’s briefing looks at the narrowing open-versus-closed model gap, the hidden economics of tokenizer changes, and how AI is moving into high-stakes operational workflows. It also reinforces the vocabulary needed to discuss model strategy, safety, and cost control in business English.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/20 [Shadowing]
item.description: Shadowing practice version — same content at a measured pace (Charon voice). For pronunciation and rhythm practice.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/20
item.description: This episode discusses the shift towards headless software and the importance of prompt governance in AI development. It covers how AI agents are increasingly interacting directly with software through APIs and structured instruction layers, impacting both software design and business models.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/19 [Shadowing]
item.description:
Shadowing practice version — same content at a measured pace (Charon voice). For pronunciation and rhythm practice.
Sources: Simon Willison — Claude system prompts as a git timeline https://github.com/simonw/research/tree/main/extract-system-prompts#readme
Hugging Face Blog — Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-ocr-v2
OpenAI News — Codex for (almost) everything https://openai.com/index/codex-for-almost-everything/
OpenAI News — The next evolution of the Agents SDK https://openai.com/index/the-next-evolution-of-the-agents-sdk/

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/19
item.description:
A What → So What → Now What briefing on prompt-governance tooling, multilingual OCR engineering, Codex's expanding desktop agent workflow, and OpenAI's more standardized agent runtime stack.
Sources: Simon Willison — Claude system prompts as a git timeline https://github.com/simonw/research/tree/main/extract-system-prompts#readme
Hugging Face Blog — Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data https://huggingface.co/blog/nvidia/nemotron-ocr-v2
OpenAI News — Codex for (almost) everything https://openai.com/index/codex-for-almost-everything/
OpenAI News — The next evolution of the Agents SDK https://openai.com/index/the-next-evolution-of-the-agents-sdk/

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/18
item.description:
A business-style AI English briefing using the Situation → Complication → Resolution framework. Today's episode covers major shifts in coding agents, AI-driven design handoff, programmable voice interfaces, reviewer-friendly agent contributions, and richer visual agent workspaces.
Sources: OpenAI News — Codex for (almost) everything https://openai.com/index/codex-for-almost-everything/ Anthropic News — Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs Google Blog — Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-1-flash-tts/ Hugging Face Blog — The PR you would have opened yourself https://huggingface.co/blog/transformers-to-mlx Cursor Changelog — Canvases https://cursor.com/changelog/04-15-26

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/17
item.description: A practical Friday briefing on how AI tooling is shifting from isolated model demos to production workflows, agent infrastructure, controllable voice generation, and higher-quality open source collaboration.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/16
item.description: A What-So-What-Now-What briefing on OpenAI's upgraded Agents SDK, GPT-5.4-Cyber access tiers, Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, and IBM's VAKRA agent benchmark.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/15
item.description: A briefing for Ken covering OpenAI's GPT-5.4-Cyber, Google's agentic coding adoption challenges, and Servo's embeddable browser engine release. The 'Before, After, Bridge' framework is used to explain the changes and connect them to actionable next steps.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/14
item.description: A business-style AI news briefing using the Problem, Solution, Impact framework, covering AI cybersecurity, software supply-chain risk, Google’s global AI push, local Gemma audio workflows, and the move toward enterprise agent systems.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/13
item.description: Today's Framework: PREP. Covers ALTK-Evolve agent learning, OpenAI enterprise AI infrastructure, the Axios supply chain incident, Anthropic Project Glasswing cybersecurity, and Safetensors joining PyTorch Foundation.
